title: Render shell rows on home: card, tap-navigation, group-header hide
action: Render cell 2's shells[] alongside the existing agents[] on the home screen. Per D1, each shell entry is its own row, same granularity as an agent card. Per D6, a shell row's primary line is its path (falling back to 'no folder yet', matching create-sheet.ts's existing phrasing) and its caption is 'Shell · <tab_label>' -- no kind watermark. Per D2, a shell row has no status badge element at all (not an empty one, not a hidden one -- simply not rendered). Per D4, shell rows participate in the existing groupByWorkspace grouping exactly like agent rows (both carry a workspace_id) -- extend groupByWorkspace's input type or add a sibling grouping call, whichever keeps the existing agent-only grouping test behavior byte-identical. Per D7, a group's header status badge is hidden entirely when every row in that group is a shell row (a client-side check: does this group contain zero agent rows -- not a check on any workspace_status value). Per D5, tapping a shell row calls the same onCreated-adjacent navigation cell 2/3 of web-create-sheet already built: construct a NewPaneRef (import from web/src/main.ts) {pane_id, workspace_id, label: <the shell row's identifying text>} and navigate to it via main.ts's existing widened Route, landing in terminal detail exactly as a just-created pane does today.

truths: A shell-only workspace's pane(s) each render as their own row on home, with path as primary line and 'Shell · <tab_label>' as caption
A shell row never renders any status badge element
2+ shell panes in the same zero-agent workspace render as 2+ separate rows

When 2+ distinct workspaces are visible and one of them is shell-only, that workspace's group header shows no status badge, while a group with at least one agent still shows its badge exactly as today
Tapping a shell row navigates into that pane's terminal detail via NewPaneRef, never fetching a full AgentRow for it
Every pre-existing switcher.test.ts assertion (agent list, grouping, collapse, refresh, logout, pull-to-refresh, the FAB from web-create-sheet) continues to pass unmodified

artifacts.substantive: renders shell rows per D1/D2/D6, groups them per D4/D7, wires tap-navigation per D5

key_links: switcher.ts imports ShellRow from api.ts and NewPaneRef from main.ts
prohibitions: Do not render a shell row inside a workspace that already shows at least one agent card (D3 -- cell 1's backend already excludes this case, but rendering code must not accidentally merge it back in)
Do not modify web/src/views/create-sheet.ts or web/src/api.ts in this cell
Do not add a new status value to the existing AgentStatus vocabulary -- a shell row has no status badge at all, per D2

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run test/switcher.test.ts

trace.dropped_reason: Merged into home-shell-workspaces-2: TypeScript's whole-project typecheck (tsconfig include: [src,test]) couples fetchAgents' signature to its sole consumer (switcher.ts) -- cell 2 could not pass its own verify without editing switcher.ts, which it was prohibited from touching, and cell 3 (the fix) was gated behind cell 2 capping. Plan-checker and cell-reviewer both independently found this deadlock and both recommended merging. Verified directly: switcher.ts:120-125 calls renderList(rows) where renderList is typed (rows: AgentRow[]) at switcher.ts:184.
